stories.headline: April 2026 sets record: 30 hacks, $625M+ stolen as DPRK fingerprints dominate
stories.tldr: Crypto's worst month for incidents in over a year saw roughly 30 separate exploits drain north of $625 million, led by Drift and KelpDAO.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Attack vectors have shifted from smart-contract bugs to social engineering and admin-key compromises, putting protocol ops teams — not auditors — on the front line.
the_details: Drift on Solana lost ~$285M April 1 in a social-engineering theft tied to North Korea's Lazarus Group.
KelpDAO's LayerZero bridge was spoofed mid-April for an estimated $293M, the largest DeFi hack of 2026 to date.
Drift and KelpDAO alone account for ~93% of April's losses; Rhea, Grinex, Volo and Sweat round out the tail.

stories.headline: Wasabi Protocol drained for $4.55M in deployer admin-key compromise
stories.tldr: Perps platform Wasabi Protocol lost about $4.55 million on April 30 after attackers gained control of its deployer admin key on Ethereum and Base.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Yet another small-cap perp DEX falls to a privileged-key compromise — a recurring pattern that's pushing institutional liquidity toward CEXs and venues with formal key management.
the_details: Funds were drained from Ethereum and Base deployments after the deployer's admin key was compromised April 30.
Wasabi paused contracts post-incident; an on-chain post-mortem is pending.
Adds to a string of April access-control failures including Volo Vault ($3.5M) and Sweat Foundation ($3.5M).

stories.headline: May token unlocks hit $2.24B as APT, STRK and ARB cluster within 96 hours
stories.tldr: About $2.24B in cliff unlocks lands between April 27 and May 31, with three major L1/L2 unlocks compressed into a four-day window mid-month.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: The May 12–16 cluster is the single most consequential window for altcoin price action this month and will test how much OTC absorption capacity actually exists.
the_details: Aptos releases ~11.3M APT (~$102M) on May 12; Starknet unlocks ~127M STRK (~$145M) on May 15.
Arbitrum follows May 16 with ~92.6M ARB (~$90M), stacking three unlocks inside 96 hours.
The May 4–10 week alone carries $753M in cliff unlocks, the heaviest single week of Q2.

stories.headline: Bitcoin pinned near $76.2K support as $110M in longs liquidated
stories.tldr: BTC is hovering above the $76,200 23.6% Fib retracement after more than $110M in leveraged longs were wiped out, with Kalshi traders pricing only a 37% chance of reclaiming $77K by tomorrow.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A break of $76.2K opens the door to $73.5K — a level that would invalidate the April uptrend and front-run the mid-May altcoin unlock wave with broad-market weakness.
the_details: Kalshi shows 64% probability BTC holds $76K by 5pm EDT tomorrow; 37% probability it reclaims $77K.
Over $110M in BTC long positions were liquidated as the Fed signals 'higher for longer' and oil rises on Iran tensions.
April closed +13% for BTC despite the late-month fade; seasonality favors May with a 13-year average return of ~8%.

stories.headline: Clarity Act 'ready for hearing' as senator signals May markup momentum
stories.tldr: The senator holding the Clarity Act says the market-structure bill is ready to move, with more momentum than ever for a May markup.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Clarity is the legislative complement to the SEC/CFTC March MOU — together they would lock in the 'most digital assets are not securities' framework before the next election cycle.
the_details: A Democrat-driven section banning government officials from personal crypto business interests remains the sticky provision.
Builds on the SEC and CFTC's March 17, 2026 joint interpretation declaring most cryptos non-securities.
TD Cowen has warned the bill could still slip to 2027 with implementation as late as 2029 if markup stalls.
